target.title: Keep codex account file reads off the menu-build path
target.body:
## Summary
Keep codex account file reads off the menu-build path: menu display now tolerates the cached account reconciliation snapshot and revalidates the cache asynchronously, instead of re-reading `auth.json`, parsing the JWT, and SHA256-fingerprinting on the main thread inside `populateMenu`.

## Context
Part of the UI hang/lag cleanup set (#1387). `populateMenu → codexAccountMenuDisplay → codexVisibleAccountProjection` loads the codex account reconciliation snapshot synchronously whenever the 2-second freshness cache has lapsed — which in practice is every menu rebuild, including rebuilds inside `menuWillOpen` and during tracking. Each load reads and hashes `auth.json` and JWT-parses the credentials on the main thread.

Field `sample` captures (1ms interval) of v0.32.5 on a real two-provider setup caught this repeatedly:

```text
# capture A: 104 samples inside populateMenu during menu tracking, of which:
28 StatusItemController.codexAccountMenuDisplay(for:)
18 SettingsStore.codexAccountReconciliationSnapshot(activeSourceOverride:)
17 DefaultCodexAccountReconciler.loadSnapshot()
17 DefaultCodexAccountReconciler.loadLiveSystemAccount()   # auth.json read + JWT parse + SHA256

# capture B (separate session): 10 samples in the same loadSnapshot subtree under populateMenu
```

## Change
- `codexAccountReconciliationSnapshotForMenuDisplay` returns the cached snapshot even past the freshness interval (active source must still match) and schedules a single coalesced cache revalidation.
- The revalidation hops through `DispatchQueue.main.async`, which AppKit does not drain while a menu-tracking run loop is active, so the reload cannot land inside an open tracking session (same deferral idea as #1388).
- Cold start (no cache) and active-source changes still load synchronously, preserving first-open correctness. All non-menu callers keep the existing 2s freshness contract.

## Validation
- `swift test --filter 'CodexAccountMenuDisplaySnapshotTests|CodexAccountReconciliationTests'` (29 tests, 2 suites, pass)
- `make check` (0 violations)
- `git diff --check`

## Runtime Proof
After: packaged release build of this branch, same machine and provider config. The menu was opened via Accessibility one refresh tick after the previous open, forcing the stale-rebuild path, while `sample CodexBar 6 1` ran:

```text
populateMenu frames: 6
reconciler/auth frames under populateMenu: 0   # grep DefaultCodexAccountReconciler|loadLiveSystemAccount|CodexAuthFingerprint
```

The reconciliation load still appears in the store's own refresh path (`UsageStore.runRefresh → currentCodexAccountScopedRefreshGuard`), which is unchanged and out of scope here — the menu-build path is clean.

## Honesty / scope notes
- The revalidation load itself still runs on the main actor — just never inside menu building or menu tracking. Account changes (e.g. re-login via CLI) surface on the next menu rebuild after the revalidation lands.
- Before-numbers are from v0.32.5; the menu-path call shape is identical on current main.
target.author: prospectore
